old_claim.note: Naval Convoy Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The corrected convoy node is scoped to Spanish treasure-fleet convoys, and the arsenal source does not make that system a direct prerequisite for standardized ship parts.

source_shape.source_locator: The Arsenale article says Venice's trade, wealth, and sea power were rooted in ships built at the Arsenale, a government-controlled factory designed to produce ships and weapons to defend trade and dominate the Mediterranean.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports Venetian arsenal ship and weapon production for Venetian trade and sea power, not a Spanish treasure-fleet prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: After the convoy node is narrowed to Spanish treasure-fleet convoys, the Venetian arsenal relationship no longer preserves the same causal boundary. The arsenal node already retains direct ship-design and technical-drawing dependencies.

ontology_before: Arsenal Standardized Ship Parts depended on a generic Naval Convoy Systems node, using a Venetian arsenal source to support a broad trade-defense context.
ontology_after: Arsenal Standardized Ship Parts no longer depends on the now-scoped Spanish Treasure Fleet Convoys node.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: arsenal_standardized_ship_parts no longer directly depends on naval_convoy_systems.
would_reject_if: The convoy node were broadened back to all state naval trade-defense systems including Venice.
A source showed Spanish treasure-fleet convoy practice directly caused Venetian-style arsenal parts standardization.
A separate generic naval trade-defense logistics node were added and sourced to the Venetian Arsenale.
short_reason: Venetian arsenal standardization is not directly caused by Spanish treasure-fleet convoys.
